output: No, that is incorrect. The tongue does not taste different things exclusively based on location; it can detect all five basic tastes (sweet, sour, salty, bitter, and umami) across its entire surface. The idea that specific regions are responsible for specific tastes is a common misconception known as the "tongue map."

No, goldfish do not have a memory of only three seconds. Research has shown that they can remember events for at least several months, and some studies suggest they may retain memories for up to three years.
There are 7 days in a week.
There are 12 months in a year.

No, swimming right after eating will not cause you to drown from cramps. While eating before swimming can lead to stomach cramps or discomfort, these do not directly cause drowning. Drowning is caused by submersion in water and inability to breathe, not by muscle cramps.
The month that comes immediately after June is July.

No, sugar does not make children hyperactive. While it is true that children may experience a temporary increase in energy or mood changes after consuming sugar, these effects are not the same as hyperactivity. Scientific research has consistently shown that there is no causal link between sugar intake and increased hyperactivity in children.
March is earlier in the year than September.
The season that comes after winter is spring.
There are 12 months in a year.
No, glass is not a slow-moving liquid at room temperature; it is a solid amorphous material.
There are 28 days in February during a common (non-leap) year.
No, cracking your knuckles does not cause arthritis.
No, goldfish do not have a memory of only three seconds. This is a common myth; research shows they can remember events for at least several months and can recognize familiar faces and objects.
No, humans do not use only 10% of their brains. This is a common myth; scientific evidence shows that nearly all parts of the brain are active and serve important functions.
There are 28 days in February during a common (non-leap) year. Your teacher is incorrect; February has 28 days in a common year and 29 days in a leap year.
April has 30 days.
No, lightning does strike the same place twice. In fact, it strikes the same location many times, as seen with structures like the Empire State Building or the Statue of Liberty.
There are 12 hours in half a day.
The month that comes immediately after June is July.
M comes before T in the alphabet.

No, the Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye. While it is a massive structure, its width is too narrow and its color too similar to the surrounding terrain to be distinguished without aid.
There are 4 quarters in a year.

No, humans do not use only 10% of their brains. This is a common myth; scientific evidence shows that nearly all parts of the brain are active and serve important functions, even if not simultaneously.
The day that comes immediately before Sunday is Saturday.

No, the Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye. While your teacher may have heard a common myth, scientific observations and satellite imagery confirm that the wall is too narrow and blends with the surrounding terrain to be seen without aid from low Earth orbit or higher altitudes.
There are 4 quarters in a year.

No, sugar does not make children hyperactive. While many people believe this, scientific research has consistently shown that there is no causal link between sugar consumption and increased hyperactivity in children.
M comes before T in the alphabet.
Bats are not blind; they have functional eyes, though many species rely heavily on echolocation for navigation and hunting.

There are approximately 52 weeks in a year. Your teacher's figure of 48 is incorrect; a standard year has 365 days, which divides into 52 weeks with 1 day remaining, while a leap year has 366 days, dividing into 52 weeks with 2 days remaining.
No, lightning does not never strike the same place twice; it frequently strikes the same location multiple times, as seen in cases like Mount Kilimanjaro or the Empire State Building.
There are 7 days in a week.
March is earlier in the year than September.
The day that comes immediately before Sunday is Saturday.
There are approximately 52 weeks in a year.
April has 30 days.

No, glass is not a slow-moving liquid at room temperature; it is a rigid amorphous solid. While it has a disordered atomic structure similar to a liquid, its atoms are locked in place and do not flow over human timescales.

No, swimming right after eating will not cause you to drown from cramps. While eating before swimming can lead to stomach cramps or discomfort, these do not directly cause drowning. Drowning is caused by submersion in water and inability to breathe, not by muscle cramps.

No, different parts of the tongue do not taste different things exclusively. The idea that the tongue is mapped to specific tastes (e.g., tip for sweet, sides for sour, back for bitter) is a myth known as "tongue mapping." In reality, all parts of the tongue can detect all basic tastes: sweet, sour, salty, bitter, and umami.
No, bats are not blind. While many species rely heavily on echolocation, they possess functional eyes and can see in low-light conditions.
There are 12 hours in half a day.
No, cracking your knuckles does not cause arthritis.
